title: AstraZeneca — Oncology — Latest Briefing
html:
AstraZeneca Oncology — Q1 2026 Briefing

The Single Most Important Thing This Quarter

Imfinzi's promotional machine is showing signs of fatigue across NSCLC indications — POSEIDON LTIP dropped 15 points (74% to 59%), message recall is declining QoQ across the board, and rep quality gaps versus Keytruda and Opdivo have widened — all while IMforte continues to chip away at the SCLC franchise. This quarter's data demands a comprehensive sales force effectiveness reset, not incremental message tweaks.

Key Developments

Imfinzi Franchise

POSEIDON — LTIP Crash and Message Recall Erosion (Q1 2026):

  • Likelihood to increase prescribing dropped significantly: 74% (Q4'25) to 59% (Q1'26), returning to Q3'25 levels
  • Average message recall declining for third consecutive quarter: 33% to 29% to 24%
  • Reps continue to prioritize STK11m (IM13) and KRASm (IM14) — these remain the only adequately recalled messages
  • Co-recall of both subgroup messages still drives 83% LTIP (+24 pts uplift) — the message combination works, but fewer reps are delivering it
  • Dosing messages (IM7, IM15) declined significantly in recall despite IM7 being one of the most differentiating messages
  • Rep quality declined: now trails Opdivo significantly on overall call quality, and Keytruda significantly on product knowledge
  • Most rep attributes declined directionally vs Q4'25, including overall call quality (now trails industry average)

PACIFIC — Stable But Misaligned (Q1 2026):

  • Overall sales call quality and LTIP remained stable, on par with industry average
  • For the first time in several quarters, messages became differentiated in recall — but prioritization is backwards:
    • NCCN guidelines (IMF26) and enduring legacy (IMF25) = most recalled but least effective
    • 66% alive at 5 years (IMF15) = most effective and motivating but significantly declined in recall (now below industry average)
    • Eligibility following CRT (IMF22) = most effective after notable increases in motivation/differentiation, but least recalled message
  • Co-recall of legacy (IMF25) + guidelines/dosing (IMF26) still drives 78% LTIP (+19 pts) — even the sub-optimal pairing has uplift
  • Positive: indication knowledge and "compelling reason to Rx" improved directionally, exceeding industry benchmarks

AEGEAN — Effectiveness Gains, Differentiation Still Elusive (Q1 2026):

  • Message effectiveness improved notably across several messages — first quarter where multiple AEGEAN messages exceeded industry average for effectiveness
  • Motivation and differentiation ratings improved across the board
  • Hazard ratio (IMF16) = most effective and motivating message, but recalled well below industry average
  • EFS (IMF15) and 2 curative intent settings (IMF10) remained the most recalled
  • BUT: AEGEAN efficacy messages still not differentiated from Keytruda's efficacy messages in the perioperative space
  • Imfinzi reps significantly trail Keytruda reps on "compelling reason to prescribe"
  • Two-thirds of MDs indicated high LTIP post-AEGEAN interaction, directionally higher vs Q4 and industry average

SCLC — IMforte Erosion Accelerates (Q1 2026):

  • Imfinzi SOV among target MDs declined to 39% (+1%) vs Tecentriq 33% (+2%) vs Zepzelca(IMforte) 25% (-3%)
  • Among all MDs: Imfinzi 38% (-3%) vs Tecentriq 36% (+2%) vs Zepzelca(IMforte) 29% (+1%)
  • IMforte message confusion worsening: MDs frequently mis-associate Luminance study (IMF11) and CNS data (IMF6) with IMforte
  • Zepzelca LTIP in 1L maintenance "improved notably and significantly led Imfinzi"
  • Most CASPIAN messages had lower effectiveness than Zepzelca's IMforte messaging
  • Bright spots: OS following cCRT (IMF2) and brain/CNS (IMF6) are among most effective SCLC messages but only recalled by ~25% of MDs
  • ADRIATIC messages continued to resonate well; LS-SCLC LTIP remained consistent and higher than industry average

Shared Sales Force Metrics (Q1 2026):

  • Imfinzi reach in NSCLC among target MDs: ~74%, stable
  • NSCLC SOV stable, leading IOs among target MDs
  • Early-stage SOV stable; metastatic SOV increased slightly
  • Imfinzi NPP SOV increased directionally; NPP reach led other IOs in most channels except direct communication
  • Tecentriq reach increased significantly among both All MDs and Target MDs — competitive intensity rising
  • Datroway NPP reach increased significantly — entering the NSCLC conversation more visibly

Beyond Lung:

  • EMERALD-3 Phase III (Imfinzi + Imjudo + lenvatinib + TACE in HCC) demonstrated statistically significant PFS improvement with trend toward OS improvement
  • Gastric/GEJ received Priority Review + Breakthrough Therapy Designation

Datroway / Dato-DXd

mBC Trajectory:

  • Datroway continues to track in HER2-/HER2-low market; no updated ZoomRx PET data this quarter beyond Feb monthly
  • Market positioning strengthened by ESMO 2025 data: Datroway outperformed Trodelvy in first global TROP2 head-to-head comparison (21% OS risk reduction in 1L TNBC vs chemo; Trodelvy failed stat sig OS in same setting)

NSCLC Presence Growing:

  • Datroway now tracked in both NSCLC SOV (from Jul'25) and NSCLC NPP baskets
  • Datroway's NPP reach increased significantly in Q1 2026 — oncologists are encountering the brand in NSCLC context through non-personal promotions
  • Methodology change: Daiichi Sankyo interactions now included for Datroway from Feb 2026 (dual promotion recognized)

1L NSCLC Pipeline:

  • Phase III trials ongoing across all PD-L1 subtypes
  • No major data readouts this quarter; next catalysts expected 2H 2026 or 2027

Company Context

  • Q1 2026 results confirmed limited tariff impact
  • Oncology revenue growth continued
  • Imfinzi on track to exceed FY2025 performance

Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Merck Keytruda ~$7.7B (consensus) +4% est. QLEX J-code (J9277) effective Apr 1; ovarian approved Feb; 3rd perioperative indication (bladder)
BMS Opdivo + Opdualag ~$2.3B (seasonal) +4% Managing toward biosimilar defense; Opdualag growing; IO breadth narrative
Gilead Trodelvy ~$400M +5% (low base) Stabilizing; pivoting to "real-world evidence" vs Datroway's "clinical trial promise"
GSK Zejula ~$60M -30% Near end of commercial life
Imfinzi On track to exceed FY2025 Growing POSEIDON LTIP dropped 15 pts; IMforte erosion accelerating
Datroway NPP reach up sig. Building NSCLC presence growing rapidly
Lynparza Declining Low single-digit PARP market consolidating

Note: Merck Q1 2026 earnings report Apr 30 — revenue is consensus estimate. BMS reported Apr 24. Gilead and GSK estimates based on trailing trends.

Merck — Keytruda (~$7.7B consensus, earnings Apr 30)

Key developments entering Q1 2026:

Keytruda's competitive pressure on Imfinzi is intensifying on three fronts simultaneously:

  1. Permanent J-code (J9277) for QLEX effective April 1, 2026: The subcutaneous pembrolizumab now has permanent billing infrastructure, removing the last administrative barrier to adoption. SC conversion protects Keytruda's installed base from biosimilar erosion AND improves patient convenience in exactly the settings where Imfinzi competes (NSCLC maintenance, perioperative). If oncologists convert existing Keytruda IV patients to SC, these patients are effectively locked into the Merck franchise with no switching opportunity for Imfinzi.

  2. Ovarian cancer approved (February 2026): Keytruda gained its latest expansion into ovarian cancer, directly overlapping with Lynparza's BRCA-mutated ovarian franchise. This creates the unusual dynamic where Merck simultaneously partners with AZ on Lynparza while competing against AZ's PARP franchise with Keytruda IO combinations in the same tumor type.

  3. Third perioperative indication — Padcev combo in bladder: Every Keytruda perioperative expansion reinforces the treatment algorithm positioning that makes AEGEAN's differentiation harder. Oncologists who prescribe Keytruda perioperatively in bladder, HNSCC, and NSCLC develop "Keytruda-first" prescribing habits across tumor types. The Q1 PET data showing Imfinzi reps significantly trailing Keytruda reps on "compelling reason to prescribe" in AEGEAN is directly connected to this franchise-wide Keytruda momentum.

  4. Keytruda LOE defense strategy is now fully visible: SC conversion + perioperative expansion + new indications (ovarian) + indication-specific data generation = a multi-layered defense that extends Keytruda's effective commercial life well beyond the 2028 LOE date. For Imfinzi, the hoped-for "post-Keytruda LOE opportunity" may be smaller than modeled.

Implication for AZ: The Keytruda threat is no longer just clinical — it's infrastructure. QLEX's permanent J-code, perioperative algorithm dominance, and ovarian expansion create a commercial ecosystem that Imfinzi cannot match on reach. AZ's counter-strategy must be data-driven differentiation in settings where Keytruda lacks superiority data: PACIFIC (Stage III, 5-year OS), ADRIATIC (LS-SCLC), and POSEIDON biomarker subgroups. The POSEIDON LTIP crash (74% to 59%) this quarter is alarming precisely because biomarker-driven messaging is Imfinzi's primary competitive lever against Keytruda.

BMS — Opdivo + Opdualag (~$2.3B seasonal, +4%)

BMS Q1 2026 earnings (April 24):

  1. BMS managing Opdivo toward biosimilar defense: BMS noted on the call: "IO competition is fierce but Opdivo's breadth and combination data sustain our position." The defensive tone signals that BMS sees the IO market as increasingly consolidated around Keytruda, with Opdivo and Imfinzi competing for the #2/#3 positions.

  2. Opdualag continuing to grow: The nivolumab + relatlimab combination maintains momentum in melanoma. BMS is building the case for IO combinations (not just IO mono) as the next era — a narrative that could benefit AZ's Imjudo (tremelimumab) combinations if the IO combo market expands.

  3. Direct competition in NSCLC, melanoma, RCC: Opdivo overlaps with Imfinzi most directly in NSCLC. The CheckMate-77T perioperative data adds another competitor in the AEGEAN setting, contributing to the "effectiveness plateau" where all IO perioperative messages cluster in a narrow range.

Implication for AZ: BMS's defensive posture creates an opportunity. As BMS shifts resources toward biosimilar defense, promotional intensity for Opdivo in active competition settings (perioperative NSCLC) may decline. Imfinzi's challenge is not Opdivo — it's Keytruda. If Opdivo's share erodes, the question is whether Imfinzi or Keytruda captures it.

Gilead — Trodelvy (~$400M, +5% from low base)

Gilead Q1 2026 (estimated, earnings pending):

  1. Trodelvy stabilizing from a lower base: After declining through 2025, Trodelvy appears to be stabilizing around ~$400M quarterly. Gilead is pivoting the brand messaging to "proven real-world evidence" versus Datroway's "clinical trial promise" — a defensive framing that acknowledges Datroway's data advantage while leveraging Trodelvy's longer commercial history.

  2. TROP2 ADC class is expanding, not zero-sum: The total addressable market for TROP2-directed therapies continues to grow as the mechanism is validated across tumor types (breast, NSCLC, urothelial). Trodelvy's stabilization reflects a floor established by indications where Datroway has less mature data, not a competitive resurgence.

  3. Datroway is capturing growth, Trodelvy is holding base: The dynamic has shifted from "Datroway takes Trodelvy share" to "Datroway captures new market growth while Trodelvy retains existing patients." This is a more favorable framing for both brands but clearly advantages Datroway for long-term positioning.

Implication for AZ: Trodelvy's stabilization means the easy share gains for Datroway in breast cancer are largely captured. Future Datroway growth comes from: (a) NSCLC expansion (where Trodelvy has no approval), (b) continued breast cancer adoption as physicians gain experience, and (c) new indications from the TROPION program. The NPP reach increase for Datroway in Q1 signals that the NSCLC growth vector is already materializing.

GSK — Zejula (~$60M, -30%)

  1. Near end of commercial life: Zejula's revenue has declined from ~$400M+ annually at peak to a ~$240M run-rate and falling. GSK has effectively exited the PARP inhibitor market.

  2. Lynparza is the last standing PARP but also declining: With Zejula gone and Rubraca (Clovis) already withdrawn, Lynparza has no direct PARP competitor. But the PARP monotherapy maintenance model is being supplanted by IO combinations and next-gen targeted therapies. Lynparza's competitive challenge is not another PARP — it's the evolution of the maintenance paradigm.

Implication for AZ: Zejula's exit removes the last PARP competitor, giving Lynparza a brief monopoly in PARP-indicated settings. But this is a declining market — AZ's investment in PARP combinations (with IO, ADC) is the only path to sustaining the franchise. The Keytruda ovarian approval creates competitive pressure from a different direction.

Synthesis: What the Combined Competitive Picture Tells Us (Q1 2026)

  1. Keytruda is building a commercial infrastructure fortress — not just winning on data. The QLEX J-code, perioperative algorithm dominance across 3+ tumor types, and ovarian expansion create an ecosystem that extends Keytruda's dominance beyond its clinical data advantages. AZ cannot out-promote this — the counter-strategy is niche data dominance in PACIFIC, ADRIATIC, and POSEIDON biomarker settings.

  2. The POSEIDON LTIP crash makes the competitive picture worse than the revenue numbers suggest. Imfinzi's revenue is growing, but the leading indicators (LTIP, rep quality vs Keytruda, message recall) are deteriorating. The competitor earnings data shows WHY: Keytruda's expanding franchise creates a gravitational pull that makes it harder for Imfinzi to differentiate even in settings where it has good data.

  3. Datroway is AZ's strongest competitive story right now. Trodelvy is stabilizing (not growing), the TROP2 class is expanding, and Datroway's NSCLC presence is building. In a quarter where Imfinzi's competitive metrics are declining, Datroway's trajectory is the most positive competitive narrative in AZ oncology.

  4. The IO market is entering a post-patent-cliff defensive era. Both Keytruda (QLEX) and Opdivo (combination strategy) are preparing for LOE. Imfinzi's newer data vintage should theoretically benefit from this — but only if AZ fixes the promotional execution issues (POSEIDON LTIP crash, rep quality gaps) that are undermining Imfinzi's data advantages.

Critical Actions / Attention Items

For AZ Client Team

  1. POSEIDON LTIP recovery plan needed — 15-point drop is not noise. Need to investigate whether message fatigue, competitive response, or rep quality decline is the primary driver
  2. PACIFIC message reprioritization — the effectiveness-recall misalignment is now backed by 3+ quarters of data. Recommend a message optimization initiative
  3. SCLC defensive strategy — IMforte confusion requires differentiated CASPIAN messaging or pivot to ADRIATIC-led storytelling. Current approach of mixing LS/ES in Team N conversations may be diluting the ADRIATIC advantage
  4. AEGEAN competitive differentiation — effectiveness gains are encouraging, but without recall of the most effective messages (hazard ratio) and with sig. gap vs Keytruda on "compelling reason to prescribe," share gains will be difficult

For ZoomRx

  1. Message optimization analysis — across all indications, build the recall-vs-effectiveness gap analysis and quantify the LTIP opportunity from reprioritization. This is a high-value deliverable
  2. SCLC brand confusion deep-dive — IMforte message mis-association data is the most actionable finding in Q1. Consider recommending an ad hoc to quantify the damage and test counter-messaging strategies
  3. Datroway NSCLC tracking upgrade — as the brand builds presence (NPP reach up significantly), the Q2 2026 PET should capture more granular Datroway NSCLC data
  4. Rep quality benchmarking — Imfinzi reps' gap vs Keytruda/Opdivo on key attributes is persistent and widening. Consider a rep training effectiveness analysis as a consultative recommendation
  5. 1L mNSCLC landscape refresh — the 2025 qualitative study is now a year old. If TROPION-Lung 1L data readout approaches, AZ will need updated quantitative landscape assessment

Quarter-over-Quarter Trend Summary

Metric Q3 2025 Q4 2025 Q1 2026 Trend
POSEIDON LTIP ~52% 74% 59% Volatile, gave back Q4 gains
POSEIDON avg recall 33% 29% 24% Declining 3 consecutive Qs
PACIFIC LTIP Stable Stable Stable Flat, on par with IA
AEGEAN LTIP ~60% ~64% ~67% Gradual improvement
SCLC Imfinzi SOV (Target) ~41% 41% 39% Slow erosion
Zepzelca(IMforte) SOV N/A 28% 25% (All)/29%(All) First two quarters; settling
ADRIATIC LTIP Building High High, consistent Strong and stable
Rep quality vs Keytruda Comparable Favorable Trailing (sig.) Deteriorating
Datroway NPP reach Launched Early Sig. increase Building rapidly

SCLC defensive strategy — IMforte confusion requires differentiated CASPIAN messaging or pivot to ADRIATIC-led storytelling. Current approach of mixing LS/ES in Team N conversations may be diluting the ADRIATIC advantage
AEGEAN competitive differentiation — effectiveness gains are encouraging, but without recall of the most effective messages (hazard ratio) and with sig. gap vs Keytruda on "compelling reason to prescribe," share gains will be difficult

For ZoomRx

Message optimization analysis — across all indications, build the recall-vs-effectiveness gap analysis and quantify the LTIP opportunity from reprioritization. This is a high-value deliverable
SCLC brand confusion deep-dive — IMforte message mis-association data is the most actionable finding in Q1. Consider recommending an ad hoc to quantify the damage and test counter-messaging strategies
Datroway NSCLC tracking upgrade — as the brand builds presence (NPP reach up significantly), the Q2 2026 PET should capture more granular Datroway NSCLC data
Rep quality benchmarking — Imfinzi reps' gap vs Keytruda/Opdivo on key attributes is persistent and widening. Consider a rep training effectiveness analysis as a consultative recommendation
